title: Bridge Essay: Exploring the Working Mechanisms between Anxiety, Mental Imagery, and Auditory Verbal Hallucinations: A Longitudinal Study.
concept: Voice-hearing — auditory verbal hallucinations on a continuum, inner speech misattribution, source monitoring, corollary discharge.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Daimon** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Voice-hearing — auditory verbal hallucinations on a continuum, inner speech misattribution, source monitoring, corollary discharge.

**Exploring the Working Mechanisms between Anxiety, Mental Imagery, and Auditory Verbal Hallucinations: A Longitudinal Study.** - Schizophr Bull Open (2025). DOI: 10.1093/schizbullopen/sgaf026. PMID: 41287756. BACKGROUND: Auditory verbal hallucinations (AVHs) are the most common hallucinations within the psychosis spectrum and are often accompanied by anxiety. Integrating mental imagery into current psychological theories may increase the understanding of working mechanisms and effectiveness of cognitive behavioral therapy for AVHs. Therefore, the current study examined associations over time between levels of anxiety, mental imagery, and AVHs in individuals with psychosis spectrum disorders. STUDY DE
